title: xylocarpus gangeticus
intro: Xylocarpus gangeticus, also known as the Gangetic mangrove, is a large tree native to the coastal regions of India, Bangladesh, and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique oval-shaped fruit and long, pointed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Xylocarpus gangeticus include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Xylocarpus gangeticus is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
